zone_6b_notes: Direct sow after last frost â€” May 1â€“15 in Farmington. Missouri summers are ideal; sunflowers tolerate heat and drought once roots are established. Succession sow every 2â€“3 weeks through June for continuous bloom. Mammoth (giant single heads, seed-harvest) and Lemon Queen (tall, multi-branch, excellent for cutting and birds) are outstanding choices. Sunflowers are allelopathic â€” avoid planting right next to vegetables. Once heads mature and droop, leave standing for birds (goldfinches, chickadees). Tall varieties need staking in Missouri wind; plant in sheltered spots or use shorter branching types.
